name: Almond Cookies #1
recipeIngredient: 32 Apricot Pits (traditional) or substitute 32 whole blanched almonds
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, cream together the lard and shortening with the granulated sugar until very light and creamy. Add the brown sugar, packed, and sifted through a coarse sieve.
2. Beat in one of the eggs and the almond extract.
3. Sift together the all-purpose flour, baking soda, and salt.
4. Gradually add about 1/3 of the dry mixture to the creamed mixture at low speed if using a mixer.
5. Press the dough together with your hands to form 2 balls, which will be very crumbly.
6. Place each ball on wax paper and roll into a cylinder about 8 inches long.
7. Chill several hours or better yet: overnight.
8. Preheat oven to 275°F. Beat the remaining egg with 2 tablespoons water.
9. Cut each cylinder into 16 equal slices using a sharp, serrated knife.
10. Place the cookies on a large ungreased baking sheet. Brush each cookie with egg wash and press an almond into the center of each.
11. Bake the cookies in the center of the oven for 25 minutes, then raise the heat to 325°F and bake for 10 to 15 minutes more, or until golden brown.
12. Transfer the cookies to a rack and let them cool completely before storing airtight for several weeks.
